entries.title: Daily drill: Lettuce 6.3 Topology Refresh Race — ElastiCache Redis Cluster MOVED Loop Blocks ML Feature Reads
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: Lettuce 6.3.0 contains a topology refresh race (GitHub #2617) where a concurrent CLUSTER NODES response during a MOVED redirect commits a partial slot→node map, sending 5 shards into a permanent MOVED oscillation loop at 340 redirects/sec during an ElastiCache Redis Cluster planned failover — 31.4% error rate, 847K sessions lose ML personalization for 28 minutes ($147K). Fix: rolling restart for immediate recovery, upgrade to Lettuce 6.3.2 for the atomic topology-snapshot fix.
items_added: Lettuce 6.3 Topology Refresh Race — ElastiCache Redis Cluster MOVED Loop Blocks ML Feature Reads for 28 Minutes During Planned Failover

entries.title: Daily drill: AWS EMR Serverless S3 Checkpoint Prefix Throttle
entries.summary: Added a Hard distributed-systems hotfix drill: S3 ListObjectsV2 rate throttling on an 8,847-object checkpoint prefix causes PySpark Structured Streaming to crash and restart from a stale checkpoint, rolling back the watermark 11 minutes and replaying historical events that overwrite a live Delta Lake feature store — 14 ML models serve stale features to 6.2M users for 31 minutes (CTR −25%, ~$168K impact). Fix covers checkpoint retention limits, UUID prefix sharding for S3 rate partitioning, batch-ID-guarded Delta merges to prevent stale overwrites, and feature-freshness alerting.
items_added: AWS EMR Serverless S3 Checkpoint Prefix Throttle — PySpark Structured Streaming Watermark Rollback Overwrites Live Feature Store for 31 Minutes

entries.title: Monday update: drill + knowledge base refresh
entries.summary: Added a Hard distributed-systems hotfix drill: Apache Pulsar BookKeeper bookie disk-full causes ledger write stall — unbounded ML feature replay job fills single bookie (4TB EBS) in 2h47m, breaking write quorum (ensemble=3, writeQuorum=2) and blocking 847 concurrent producers across 14 ML feature pipelines ($1.2M/hr impact); fix involves emergency bookie scale-out, ledger GC to free disk, and rate-limiting the replay job. Knowledge base refresh: added vLLM v0.25.0 (Model Runner V2 default, Streaming Parser Engine for unified tool-call parsing, DSpark/DFlash universal speculative decoding) to ml-systems; Uber Cart Assistant 8-stage state-graph architecture (LLMs for ambiguity, deterministic systems for retrieval/pricing/validation — 34% cart abandonment reduction) to system-design; LangChain State of Agent Engineering 2026 (57% orgs in production, quality top barrier, observability-vs-evals gap) to org-decisions; and three new tech talks: ICML 2026 Pascale Fung on world-model-grounded agents, AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1 (Software Factories, Microsoft/OpenAI/HF) and Day 2 (Autoresearch, Anthropic/DeepMind/Amazon AGI).

entries.title: Daily drill: SageMaker Real-Time Endpoint Scale-to-Zero — MinCapacity=0 Kills Both Instances During CDN Reconnect Valley
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: a cost-optimization ticket set MinCapacity=0 on a SageMaker real-time endpoint's Application Auto Scaling policy 6 weeks ago without incident review. A 4-minute, 19-second CDN reconnect backoff valley drives InvocationsPerInstance to 0 for 5 consecutive minutes, triggering scale-in to 0 instances at 04:22:00 UTC; 7 seconds later, 214,847 concurrent Lambda invocations hit the endpoint in 'Updating' state -- 100% ServiceUnavailable for 3 minutes 42 seconds and $84K revenue impact. Covers CloudTrail scale-in triage, MinCapacity=1 immediate fix, Lambda retry logic for ServiceUnavailable, ScaleInCooldown tuning to 900s, CloudWatch Synthetics canary, and CI gate blocking MinCapacity=0 on production endpoints.
items_added: SageMaker Real-Time Endpoint Scale-to-Zero — MinCapacity=0 Kills Both Instances During 4-Minute CDN Reconnect Valley, 3m 42s Complete Outage for 214K Users

entries.title: Daily drill: PostgreSQL Logical Replication Slot WAL Bloat — Debezium CDC OOM Loop Fills RDS Disk
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty database hotfix drill: Debezium 2.5 CDC consumer on ECS OOM-crashes 5 times during a bulk 18M-row COPY, leaving the PostgreSQL logical replication slot frozen and retaining 18.2 GB of WAL — exhausting RDS disk and crashing the primary for 8 minutes. Covers replication slot diagnosis, emergency slot drop, safe Debezium heap tuning, WAL lag CloudWatch alerting via Lambda, and pre-flight bulk-operation guardrails.
items_added: PostgreSQL Logical Replication Slot WAL Bloat — Debezium CDC Consumer OOM Loop Pins 18 GB of WAL, Fills RDS Disk, Crashes Primary for 8 Minutes

entries.title: Daily drill: gRPC HTTP/2 GOAWAY Frame Storm During Rolling Deployment
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty API/service-mesh hotfix drill: recommendation-svc rolling deployment (v3.1.4 → v3.1.5) adds gRPC keepalive with GRPC_ARG_KEEPALIVE_PERMIT_WITHOUT_CALLS=true, causing long-lived idle H2 connections to persist to Terminating pods; Envoy's 5s preStop is shorter than its drain window, so GOAWAY frames arrive while clients hold active RPCs — spiking UNAVAILABLE errors to 8.3% (1,847/min peak) for ~30s per pod termination. Fix: preStop sleep 35s, minReadySeconds 30, gRPC client retry on UNAVAILABLE, and Envoy drain_duration 45s via Istio EnvoyFilter.
items_added: gRPC HTTP/2 GOAWAY Frame Storm During Rolling Deployment — Keepalive-Pinned Idle Connections Absorb Envoy GOAWAY on Pod Termination, 8.3% Unavailable Error Spike

entries.title: Daily drill: NATS JetStream MaxAckPending Saturation — Sequential AI Inference Latency Spike Silently Stalls Go ML Pipeline for 23 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Go ML event pipeline's NATS JetStream PullSubscribe consumer uses sequential inference calls (50 msgs × 28.3s P99 each = 23.6 min per batch), causing all MaxAckPending=1,000 slots to fill with redelivery-looping messages when a vLLM rolling restart spikes inference P99 past the AckWait=30s threshold. NATS stops delivering new messages; the consumer logs 'no messages' and masks 847,293 queued events, causing 23 minutes of feed staleness and $94K revenue impact. Fix: bounded goroutine pool with InProgress() heartbeats to extend AckWait during slow inference.
items_added: NATS JetStream MaxAckPending Saturation — Sequential AI Inference Latency Spike Pins Consumer Ack Window, Silently Stalls Go ML Pipeline for 23 Minutes

entries.title: Daily drill: Azure Container Apps Blue-Green Deploy Exhausts MySQL max_connections — 23% Checkout Error Rate for 7 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: an Azure Container Apps blue-green deploy of a Java Spring Boot service temporarily runs 2 full revisions (30 replicas each × HikariCP maximumPoolSize=20 = 1,200 total connections) against a MySQL 8.0 Flexible Server GP_Standard_D8ds_v4 whose max_connections is 800, causing both revisions to fail simultaneously with ERROR 1040 (HY000): Too many connections for 7 minutes, 23.4% checkout error rate, and $142K in failed transactions.
items_added: Azure Container Apps Blue-Green Deploy Doubles HikariCP Connection Pool — MySQL 8.0 Flexible Server max_connections Exhausted, 23% Checkout Error Rate for 7 Minutes

entries.title: Daily drill: Apache Airflow XCom PostgreSQL Bloat — ML Training Scheduler Stall Delays 847 Batch Jobs 22 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a data scientist's ml_training_dag pushes 174MB pandas DataFrames as JSON into the Airflow XCom PostgreSQL table, accumulating 47.4M dead tuples (96.4% bloat) over 4.5 weeks. Autovacuum's 22-minute VACUUM FREEZE on the xcom table holds an AccessExclusiveLock that stalls all 3 Airflow scheduler pods, causing 847 ML batch jobs to expire their SQS timeout, blocking the downstream inference_pipeline_dag, and serving 4.2M users 6-hour-stale recommendations with $314K revenue impact.
items_added: Apache Airflow XCom PostgreSQL Bloat — ML Training DAG Serializes 174MB DataFrames to Metadata DB, Scheduler Stall Delays 847 Batch Jobs 22 Minutes, Stale Recommendations to 4.2M Users

entries.title: Daily drill: Azure Service Bus Session Lock Expiry — .NET 8 ServiceBusProcessor Stalls Entire User Session, 12,847 Message Backlog in 28 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: an Azure Service Bus Premium session-enabled queue accumulates 12,847 unprocessed messages in 28 minutes after a .NET 8 ServiceBusProcessor's gRPC TorchServe call (NC6s_v3 GPUs) exceeds the 60-second MessageLockDuration, causing AbandonMessageAsync() to throw a lock-expired exception that silently halts the entire per-user session — blocking all subsequent messages due to FIFO ordering. Exercise covers Azure Monitor triage, local reproduction with emulator, lock-renewal hotfix via RenewMessageLockAsync(), backlog drain with session re-keying, gRPC deadline enforcement, and hardening with PT5M lock duration.
items_added: Azure Service Bus Session Lock Expiry — .NET 8 ServiceBusProcessor Stalls Entire User Session, ML Pipeline Backlog 12,847 Messages in 28 Minutes

entries.title: Daily drill: cert-manager CertificateRequest Backlog — Vault PKI Saturation Blocks All GKE Pod Scheduling for 23 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: a data scientist deploys MLflow experiment pods with 1-minute cert TTLs on GKE (Istio 1.22.1 / cert-manager 1.14.7 / Vault 1.17.0), generating 32.4 Vault PKI sign requests/second that saturate the shared PKI connection pool. After 37 minutes of starvation, the Istio sidecar injector webhook's TLS cert expires — its failurePolicy: Fail causes 100% cluster-wide pod scheduling failure across all namespaces for 23 minutes, stalling 38 ML training jobs and a payment service security rollout ($190K impact). Exercise covers MPS blast-radius triage via simultaneous multi-namespace FailedCreate events, failurePolicy: Ignore as immediate unblock, MLflow scale-to-zero to clear Vault backlog, istiod cert restoration, and OPA policy to enforce minimum 5-minute cert TTL.
items_added: cert-manager CertificateRequest Backlog — 1-Minute MLflow Cert TTL Saturates Vault PKI, Istiod Webhook TLS Expires, Blocks All GKE Pod Scheduling Cluster-Wide for 23 Minutes

entries.title: Daily drill: AWS Inferentia2 Neuron Compiled Artifact Cache Collision
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: AWS Inferentia2 serving container loads a stale June 9 .neff artifact (512K-vocab) instead of the June 16 retrained binary (524K-vocab) because the S3 cache key used only model_name+version_tag — silently clamping 12,288 new product SKU embedding indices to the padding token for 14 days with 0% HTTP error rate and $412K GMV opportunity cost. Exercise covers neuron-ls artifact timestamp triage, rolling instance replacement to force recompilation, content-hash-based cache keys, vocab-range probe deployment gate, and neuron_model_vocab_size Prometheus guard.
items_added: AWS Inferentia2 Neuron Compiled Artifact Cache Collision — Stale .neff Serves 14-Day-Old Model Weights, Silent Wrong Outputs for 12K New Products

entries.title: Daily drill: Azure Service Bus Session Lock Zombie — Pod OOMKill Triggers acceptNextSession 429 Storm
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: an AKS node drain OOMKills a Java pod mid-session, leaving an Azure Service Bus session lock held by the dead pod for 5 minutes while 15 survivor pods simultaneously hammer acceptNextSession — generating 900 throttled 429 requests/min that escalate to a 60-second backoff tier, halting all ordered order processing for 14 minutes (19,830 queued orders, $87K SLA exposure). Exercise covers zombie session lock triage, rolling-restart to reset backoff state, startup jitter fix, Session Lock Duration reduction from 5min to 60s, and Azure Monitor alerts for the throughput/active-session divergence pattern.

entries.title: Daily drill: GCP Cloud Bigtable Timestamp-Prefix Row Key Hotspot — Sequential Write Concentration Saturates Tablet Servers
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty database hotfix drill: a GCP Cloud Bigtable feature store for 3 ML recommendation models experiences 20× SLO breach (P99 4,210ms vs 200ms target) when a marketing campaign 2.1× write-rate increase pushes 2 of 6 tablet servers to 96% CPU. Root cause is the classic timestamp-prefix anti-pattern — monotonically increasing keys concentrate 100% of current writes on the same lexicographic range. Exercise covers Key Visualizer hotspot triage, Dataflow throttle as immediate mitigation, reversed-timestamp key redesign for balanced distribution with per-user recency ordering, and zero-downtime dual-write migration to the new table.
items_added: GCP Cloud Bigtable Timestamp-Prefix Row Key Hotspot — Sequential Write Concentration Saturates Tablet Servers, Feature Store P99 Latency 20× SLO

entries.title: Daily drill: Kafka Consumer Livelock — max.poll.interval.ms Breach During Downstream Throttle
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Java Kafka consumer group on AWS EKS enters an infinite livelock when a SendGrid API slowdown (48ms → 11.2s) causes batch processing to exceed max.poll.interval.ms=300s. entries.title: Daily drill: MySQL InnoDB Gap Lock Deadlock Storm — Inventory Reservation Cascade During Flash Sale
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty MySQL 8.0 hotfix drill: a Java Spring Boot inventory service hits a 34.7% error rate when InnoDB gap locks on an out-of-stock range query deadlock with Insert Intention Locks from concurrent order inserts during a flash sale (8,400 concurrent writes). Covers exact INNODB STATUS deadlock output, READ COMMITTED isolation fix, SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED pattern, and retry amplification mitigation.
items_added: MySQL InnoDB Gap Lock Deadlock Storm — Inventory Reservation Cascade During Flash Sale

entries.title: Daily drill: Apache Flink RocksDB Checkpoint Timeout — L0 Compaction I/O Starvation on Feature Pipeline
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed systems hotfix drill: a Flink 1.18.1 streaming feature pipeline (32 TMs on GKE, RocksDB state backend) experiences 3 consecutive checkpoint failures when an upstream Pub/Sub publisher quadruples event batch size (128KB→512KB), triggering simultaneous L0 compaction across all 32 TaskManagers and saturating PD-SSD write bandwidth at 1.78 GB/s. Flink restarts from a 13-minute-old checkpoint, serving stale features to 14 ML ranking models for 14 minutes. Exercise covers RocksDB L0 stall triage via Flink Prometheus metrics, compaction thread throttle as immediate mitigation, upstream batch size reversion, local NVMe migration, and managed memory configuration.
items_added: Apache Flink RocksDB Checkpoint Timeout — L0 Compaction I/O Starvation on Feature Pipeline

entries.title: Daily drill: vLLM Speculative Decoding Draft Model Vocab Mismatch — Corrupted Responses After Target Model Rollout
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill on GKE: vLLM 0.4.2 speculative decoding returns HTTP 200 but only 7–9 token truncated responses after a target model upgrade expands the tokenizer vocab from 32K to 36K while the draft model remains at 32K. A vLLM engine off-by-one bug in the spec-decode rejection rollback loop corrupts the output buffer pointer on last-token rejections. Exercise covers spec-decode metric triage (acceptance_rate→0.00), disabling spec-decode as immediate mitigation, upgrading to vLLM 0.4.3, and adding ArgoCD PreSync vocab-parity hooks and synthetic quality canaries.
items_added: vLLM Speculative Decoding Draft Model Vocab Mismatch — Corrupted Responses After Target Model Rollout

entries.title: Daily drill: Tokio Ack Handler Shutdown Race — Pub/Sub Redelivery Storm During GKE Rolling Update
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Rust/Tokio Pub/Sub consumer on GKE causes a 340K-message redelivery storm when fire-and-forget tokio::spawn ack tasks are dropped by Tokio's shutdown_timeout during a rolling update, leaving 847 messages unacknowledged per pod across 8 simultaneous pod terminations. Exercise covers structured concurrency fix via JoinSet, SIGTERM drain guard, terminationGracePeriodSeconds tuning, and CI enforcement via Pub/Sub emulator integration tests.
items_added: Tokio Ack Handler Shutdown Race — Pub/Sub Redelivery Storm During GKE Rolling Update

entries.title: Daily drill: Elasticsearch Disk Flood Stage — force_merge Segment Merge Overhead Triggers Mass Index Read-Only Lockout
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: an automated force_merge on 280GB of aging Elasticsearch indices generates temporary merge-overhead disk usage that, combined with a G1GC Full GC pause deferring segment release, crosses the 95% flood-stage watermark and applies read_only_allow_delete=true to all indices cluster-wide. Standard cluster health shows GREEN throughout; 847 writes/sec return HTTP 403 for 6 minutes with 304,920 events dropped before the on-call discovers the cause. Exercise covers index-block triage via settings API (invisible to standard health checks), immediate block-clear, safe force_merge strategy, and automated flood-stage remediation Lambda.
items_added: Elasticsearch Disk Flood Stage — force_merge Segment Merge Overhead Triggers Mass Index Read-Only Lockout

entries.title: Daily drill: SQS Visibility Timeout Race — Lambda Duration Spike Causes Duplicate Payment Charges During Flash Sale
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: AWS Lambda Node.js payment processor on SQS has its Duration P99 pushed from 17s to 31s when Stripe degrades to 1.2s and a SageMaker fraud endpoint saturates at 9.8s P99 during a flash sale — exceeding the 30s SQS VisibilityTimeout. Combined with an idempotency ordering bug (Stripe called before the DynamoDB conditional write), 847 customers are double-charged ($158,389 total). Exercise covers X-Ray dependency triage, VisibilityTimeout increase, Lambda concurrency throttle, idempotency-first reordering with DynamoDB PutItem as a distributed mutex, and Stripe native idempotency keys as defense-in-depth.
items_added: SQS Visibility Timeout Race — Lambda Duration Spike Causes Duplicate Payment Charges During Flash Sale

entries.title: Daily drill: Vault Dynamic Secret max_ttl Reduction — Synchronized Lease Expiry Causes Mass PostgreSQL Auth Failure
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: a Vault operator shortens the PostgreSQL secrets engine max_ttl from 24h to 1h on production roles, causing all 96 Go service pods' vault-agent leases to expire simultaneously 1 hour later. The Go pgx v5 pool is static (initialized once at startup) and doesn't reinitialize when vault-agent writes new credentials, leaving all 12 services unable to connect to RDS PostgreSQL for 22 minutes. Exercise covers credential-vs-network triage, rolling restart mitigation, fsnotify-based hot credential rotation fix, and Vault lease staggering prevention.
items_added: Vault Dynamic Secret max_ttl Reduction — Synchronized Lease Expiry Causes Mass PostgreSQL Auth Failure

entries.title: Daily drill: Azure AKS CoreDNS ndots:5 Storm — DNS Search Domain Amplification Kills Kafka and Service Mesh Connectivity
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: on Azure AKS, Kubernetes ndots:5 default causes 180 Java pods to generate 28,512 upstream DNS queries/sec (28× the Azure Private DNS Resolver quota of 1,000 QPS) after a KEDA scale-out for a product launch, killing Kafka connectivity and exhausting HikariCP database connection pools. Covers FQDN trailing-dot immediate fix, ndots:1 dnsConfig as durable solution, CoreDNS NXDOMAIN alerting, and NodeLocal DNSCache for long-term DNS throughput scaling.
items_added: Azure AKS CoreDNS ndots:5 Storm — DNS Search Domain Amplification Kills Kafka and Service Mesh Connectivity

entries.title: Daily drill: RabbitMQ Quorum Queue Memory Alarm — Celery Prefetch Surge OOM Wave Blocks All Pika Producers on GKE
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a Celery `worker_prefetch_multiplier` bump from 100→2000 causes 4 of 24 GKE worker pods to OOMKill, returning 8,000 unacked messages to RabbitMQ and triggering a memory alarm (41.8% of 64 GB) that blocks all 14 Pika producer connections. The coordinator's `wait_for_confirms_or_die(timeout=5.0)` treats the transient block as a fatal error, causing 100% error rate for 17 minutes; a misrouted liveness probe cascades into a coordinator pod restart storm. Exercise covers memory-alarm triage via `rabbitmqctl`, Celery scale-to-zero as immediate relief, `CELERYD_PREFETCH_MULTIPLIER=1` for long-running tasks, Pika `Connection.Blocked` callback handling, lazy queue mode to decouple depth from RAM, and prefetch guard at container startup.
items_added: RabbitMQ Quorum Queue Memory Alarm — Celery worker_prefetch_multiplier Surge Triggers OOM Wave and Blocks All Pika Producers on GKE

entries.title: Daily drill: Temporal Workflow Sticky Queue Drain Stall — ML Training Pipeline Timeout Cascade During Worker Rolling Restart
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a dormant Go SDK misconfiguration (StickyScheduleToStartTimeout=0 maps to infinite wait, not 'disabled') causes 92 Temporal TrainModelWorkflow instances to hang on terminated-pod sticky queues during a GKE 1.28→1.29 rolling restart. Workflows time out after 5 minutes, while their orphaned A100-80GB training activities continue burning $32.77/hr each — accumulating $17,499 in orphaned GPU compute before the on-call terminates them. Exercise covers sticky queue triage via tctl, orphaned GPU termination, StickyScheduleToStartTimeout fix (set to 10s or omit for server default), bulk workflow reset, and a pre-rolling-restart queue-depth gate in CI/CD.
items_added: Temporal Workflow Sticky Queue Drain Stall — ML Training Pipeline Timeout Cascade During Worker Rolling Restart

entries.title: Daily drill: TGI Streaming Zombie Sessions — iOS Background TCP Suspension Exhausts KV Cache Pool
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty ML Serving hotfix drill: iOS 4.2.0 ships a background-fetch feature that suspends TCP sockets without sending FIN, causing TGI 2.1.0 Rust streaming tasks to block indefinitely on poll_write (EAGAIN). Over 4h 14min, 2,847 zombie SSE sessions accumulate and exhaust the entire 8.5 GB A10G KV cache pool on SageMaker, producing 89.3% error rate with near-zero GPU utilization. Exercise covers zombie-session triage via GPU memory vs utilization disparity, SageMaker endpoint cycling as immediate mitigation, TGI 2.2.0 --client-idle-timeout fix, iOS app TCP FIN remediation, and a compound CloudWatch alarm targeting the unique high-VRAM/low-compute fingerprint.
items_added: TGI Streaming Zombie Sessions — iOS Background TCP Suspension Exhausts KV Cache Pool

entries.title: Daily drill: Azure Event Hubs Partition Ownership Thundering Herd — HPA Oscillation Triggers Blob Storage Throttle
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: an HPA misconfiguration (stabilizationWindowSeconds: 0) on an Azure AKS feature ingestion service triggers 7 rapid scale oscillations (8→48→8 pods), causing 40 simultaneous pod terminations that release all Event Hubs partition ownership blobs at once. The 8 surviving pods race on ETag-based blob claims, generating 31,200 blob requests/min (78× baseline), saturating Azure Blob Storage throughput limits, and halting all consumer progress for 22 minutes while 4.2M events accumulate. Exercise covers ownership storm triage via Azure Monitor throttle metrics, HPA freeze as immediate mitigation, BlobCheckpointStore convergence monitoring, and prevention via HPA scale-in stabilization window and TableCheckpointStore migration.

All 14 SLO monitors enter silent 'No Data' state; 35 minutes later a real vLLM 8.3% 503 error spike goes undetected until a customer Slack message. entries.title: Daily drill: Kubernetes etcd WAL Fsync Stall — EBS Volume I/O Impairment Triggers Leader Election Storm, 19 GPU Nodes NotReady
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: a self-managed Kubernetes 1.28 cluster's etcd FSyncs balloon from 2ms to 3,847ms when an AWS EBS gp2 volume (secretly downgraded from io1 in a cost-optimization Terraform commit) suffers a 4-minute I/O impairment. 12 Raft leader-election cycles destabilize the API server (31.4% error rate), 19 of 24 A100 GPU worker nodes go NotReady, and 14 PyTorch DDP training jobs deadlock in NCCL AllReduce — burning $12,800 in wasted GPU compute. Exercise covers fsync-latency triage via etcd metrics, live io2 volume migration, etcd election-timeout hardening, and Terraform Sentinel policy to block future gp2 regressions.
items_added: Kubernetes etcd WAL Fsync Stall — EBS Volume I/O Impairment Triggers Leader Election Storm, 19 GPU Nodes NotReady

entries.title: Daily drill: AWS RDS Proxy Connection Pinning Storm — SET search_path in Multi-Tenant Lambda Functions Exhausts Proxy Pool
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty database hotfix drill: a multi-tenant SaaS checkout service's Node.js Lambda functions use SET search_path TO tenant_${tenantId} in pg pool.on('connect'), causing RDS Proxy to pin every connection. Under normal load (50 Lambdas) this is invisible for 7 months; when end-of-month billing fires 612 concurrent Lambdas, all 400 proxy connections are pinned simultaneously, producing 71.4% checkout error rate for 19 minutes and $3.2M in unprocessed billing. Fix replaces the SET command with PostgreSQL connection string options=-csearch_path=... startup parameter, which RDS Proxy does not pin.
items_added: AWS RDS Proxy Connection Pinning Storm — SET search_path in Multi-Tenant Lambda Functions Exhausts Proxy Pool During End-of-Month Billing Run

entries.title: Daily drill: Grafana Loki Ingester OOM — High-Cardinality tenant_id Label Silences All Alertmanager Rules for 52 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty infrastructure hotfix drill: a Java Spring Boot recommendation service on GKE moves tenant_id from a JSON log body field to a Loki structured label, exploding from 48 to 393,600 active Loki streams and OOMKilling all 6 ingesters at 09:31 UTC. All 14 Alertmanager log-based rules silently enter 'pending' (no data) state; an ML inference regression goes undetected for 52 minutes until a customer reports it via Slack. Exercise covers cardinality triage via logcli and Prometheus, rollback-and-redeploy mitigation, Loki ingester recovery, correct per-tenant instrumentation via LogQL structured parsing plus a Prometheus metrics stage, and prevention via stream-creation-rate alerting, max_streams_per_user limits, CI cardinality linting, and an Alertmanager DeadMansSwitch heartbeat.
items_added: Grafana Loki Ingester OOM — High-Cardinality tenant_id Label Silences All Alertmanager Rules for 52 Minutes

entries.title: Daily drill: DynamoDB Hot Partition — Sequential User ID Batch Scan Exhausts Adaptive Capacity, Stales ML Feature Reads for 2.3M Users
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a daily batch inference pipeline reads DynamoDB user features in sequential user_id order (usr_00000001…usr_02300000), causing a hash-collision hotspot where 63% of IDs land on 3 internal partitions. At 71,204 RCU/min (12× provisioned), Adaptive Capacity burst credits exhaust in 15 minutes — throttling the real-time recommendation Lambda and serving 6-hour-stale features to 2.3M DAUs, dropping feed CTR 28% and costing $184K/hr. Exercise covers Contributor Insights triage, Lambda concurrency freeze as immediate mitigation, batch read shuffle + adaptive boto3 retry as the fix, and SQS-spread BatchGetItem for durable rate control.

Covers MPS blast-radius triage, per-tenant MPS server isolation, flash-attn upgrade fix, and CUDA exception CI validation gate.

entries.title: Daily drill: Kafka KRaft Controller Voter Set Mismatch — PVC Deletion Blocks Topic Mutations for 27 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: on a GKE Confluent Platform 7.6 cluster running KRaft mode, the on-call SRE applies the (safe-in-ZooKeeper) PVC-delete-and-recreate procedure to a corrupted controller disk — but in KRaft mode this wipes meta.properties, leaving the replacement pod in CrashLoopBackOff and the 2-node quorum unable to commit any controller-plane log entry. All AdminClient mutations (CreateTopics, AlterConfigs) timeout for 27 minutes, stalling 14 microservice canary deploys and Confluent Schema Registry. Fix: kafka-storage.sh format with the surviving cluster UUID, then pod delete to restart cleanly.
items_added: Kafka KRaft Controller Voter Set Mismatch — PVC Deletion Prevents Replacement Controller from Joining Quorum, Blocks All Topic Mutations for 27 Minutes

entries.title: Friday update: drill + market signal scan
entries.summary: Added a Hard ML Serving hotfix drill: Istio/Envoy per-instance retry budget amplifies a 3.2% vLLM KV cache 503 error rate into 100% outage in 4 minutes — retries hold KV blocks for 30s perTryTimeout, preventing recovery; fix is disable retries on 5xx, switch to cluster-wide retry budget via EnvoyFilter, and use vLLM 429 load shedding to structurally break the feedback loop. Market scan: SpaceX/xAI acquires Cursor for $60B (largest AI developer tools M&A ever); Google DeepMind mass researcher exodus (Noam Shazeer → OpenAI, John Jumper → Anthropic); Alphabet $80B equity raise for $180-190B 2026 AI capex; OpenAI confidential S-1 filed June 8. Updated Cursor watchlist entry to reflect acquisition; added OpenAI to watchlist.

entries.title: Daily drill: Nomad Job Priority Inversion — ML Training Batch priority=100 Preempts All 24 Payment Service Allocations, $1.54M Revenue Halt in 22 Minutes
entries.summary: Added a Hard-difficulty distributed-systems hotfix drill: a data scientist deploys an ml-training-sweep batch job with priority=100 (copy-pasted from dev cluster without review) to a prod HashiCorp Nomad 1.7.6 cluster running at 87% capacity. The payment-api service runs at priority=80. Nomad's batch preemption evicts all 24 payment-api allocations in 2 seconds; a priority-inversion death cycle re-preempts any rescheduled payment-api alloc every 3 seconds, Consul deregisters the service, Envoy upstreams go empty, and 312K sessions receive 503s for 22 minutes ($1.54M revenue halted). Exercise covers identification via nomad job status, stopping the batch job, fixing priority tiers (batch=30, payment=95), enforcing a Nomad Sentinel job-policy, isolating ML training in a separate namespace with preemption disabled, and prevention via CI linter, PagerDuty preemption alert, and a dev-to-prod migration checklist.
items_added: Nomad Job Priority Inversion — ML Training Batch priority=100 Preempts All 24 Payment Service Allocations, $1.54M Revenue Halt in 22 Minutes
